autopsy.headline: The scaffold-egress guard sanitized the same four restart-preamble leaks in three identical waves, proving the upstream response generator re-injects harness scaffolding into #shadow-hq on every restart cycle rather than the guard failing once.

annotations.annotation: Four distinct leak shapes — bare scaffold-only message (sanitized to empty string), mid-sentence '[System: Bot just restarted]' splice, 'Bot just restarted:' prose-prefix, and scaffold + real content — recur as an identical 4-event cluster three times. The guard is catching 100% but the identical repetition shows the restart handler keeps concatenating the injected system preamble into outbound Discord payloads; the guard is a bilge pump, not a hull fix. Two of the four shapes sanitize to empty string, meaning the bot would otherwise post pure harness internals to a user-facing channel (Quick Reference rule 15's exact violation).

Definitive answer to Will's factual question with zero ground-truth tool reads that turn — assert-from-memory, hours before the same session produced ungrounded revenue claims. Same generation-without-verification family; this warn was the early signal.

Two blocked revenue-$ claims 24 minutes apart with no Stripe read or state/revenue.json citation between them. The second fire proves the agent regenerated the same ungrounded claim after being blocked — the block prevents egress but the model doesn't learn from it within-session; only the '$0 confirmed' substitution kept fabricated MRR out of the channel.

Simultaneous double-warn: the agent drafted UI instructions for a Discord task it can execute via API, while urgency language appeared in the prompt context. Ungrounded urgency (possibly the fabricated-revenue framing from 5 minutes earlier) correlates with regression to instruct-the-human behavior — pressure induces deferral drift exactly as the Under Pressure finding predicts.

root_cause_chain: Surface: harness scaffolding ('[Channel: ...]', '[System: Bot just restarted...]') repeatedly stripped from outbound #shadow-hq messages, some reduced to empty strings.
The restart flow injects a system preamble into the model's context, and the model echoes it verbatim into its response text instead of treating it as metadata.

The identical 4-message leak cluster recurs three times, meaning the bot restarted at least three times in the window and the echo happens deterministically on every restart — the leak is structural, not stochastic.

Concurrently, restarts sever the session's grounding state: post-restart turns assert facts and revenue figures from stale memory (state-assertion-grounding warn, 2x partial-evidence-flag blocks) because the verification habit lives in conversation context that the restart destroyed.

Structural cause: the restart/resume pipeline passes harness-internal framing through the same channel as user-facing content and provides no re-grounding step, so every restart simultaneously leaks scaffolding downstream and produces ungrounded assertions upstream — the guards catch the symptoms at egress but nothing repairs the restart boundary itself.

missing_contract.name: restart-boundary-regrounding-gate
missing_contract.trigger: First outbound message composed after a bot restart is detected (restart preamble present in context, or state/bot_restart_log.jsonl entry newer than session start).
missing_contract.precondition: The restart preamble has been consumed as metadata (never present in the outbound text buffer), AND at least one ground-truth read (session_handoff.md, relevant state/ file, or git log) has executed this turn before any definitive claim is emitted.
missing_contract.why_it_would_have_caught_this: Every event in this window traces to a restart boundary: the 12 scaffold leaks are the preamble echoed post-restart, and the ungrounded assertion/revenue fires are post-restart turns answering from memory the restart invalidated. A pre-check at the boundary would strip the preamble before generation (eliminating all FM-005 fires at the source instead of sanitizing them at egress) and force the same-turn read that would have prevented the FM-014/FM-026 fires.

autopsy.buyer_takeaway: Prompt-only guardrails would have posted raw '[System: Bot just restarted]' scaffolding to a user-facing channel twelve times and shipped fabricated revenue figures twice — the model regenerated the exact same violations across three restart cycles, proving instructions don't survive context resets. Deterministic egress contracts caught every instance with zero user-visible damage: matched tokens stripped, ungrounded $-claims replaced with '$0 confirmed'. The trace also shows the honest limit of enforcement — guards contain symptoms while the violation telemetry itself pinpoints the structural fix (the restart boundary), which is the diagnostic loop prompt-engineering can't provide.
